title: Coccoloba lindeniana
taxonomy: Coccoloba lindeniana is classified within the family Polygonaceae. This family of plants is commonly known as buckwheat or smartweed family. The genus Coccoloba is native to the Americas, and C. lindeniana is a species within this genus. Coccoloba lindeniana is a type of coastal shrub or small tree. It is native to the Caribbean and parts of Central America.

intro: Coccoloba lindeniana, also known as the Panama buttonwood or West Indian sea grape, is a species of flowering plant in the family Polygonaceae, native to the Caribbean and Central America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Coccoloba lindeniana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Coccoloba lindeniana include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections.
